title: Welcome to the Engineering Knowledge Base
content:
Overview

This knowledge base is the single source of truth for all engineering documentation at our company. It contains architecture guides, coding standards, onboarding materials, and operational runbooks.

How to Use This Knowledge Base

Use the search bar at the top to find articles by keyword. You can also browse by category using the sidebar navigation. Each article includes a table of contents for quick navigation within longer documents.

Contributing

All engineers are encouraged to contribute to the knowledge base. If you find outdated information or want to add a new article, contact the platform team or use the Admin Panel to upload a document directly.

Contribution Guidelines

  • Keep articles concise and actionable
  • Use clear headings to structure content
  • Add relevant tags to improve discoverability
  • Review articles at least quarterly

Support

For questions about the knowledge base platform itself, reach out to the #platform-engineering Slack channel.
excerpt: This knowledge base is the single source of truth for all engineering documentation. Learn how to navigate, search, and contribute.
author: Platform Team

title: Code Review Process and Standards
content:
Purpose

Code review is one of the most important practices in our engineering culture. This document outlines how we conduct reviews, what we look for, and the standards all engineers must follow.

Review Process

Every pull request must be reviewed by at least one other engineer before merging. For changes that affect core infrastructure or security, two reviews are required.

Opening a Pull Request

  • Write a clear, descriptive title
  • Fill out the PR template completely
  • Link to the relevant Jira ticket
  • Add screenshots for UI changes
  • Keep PRs small and focused — aim for under 400 lines of change

Reviewing a PR

  • Respond within one business day
  • Be constructive and specific in feedback
  • Distinguish between blocking issues and suggestions
  • Approve only when you are genuinely satisfied

Code Standards

All code must pass our automated linting and test suite before it can be merged. TypeScript strict mode is enforced across all frontend projects. Backend services must have at least 80% test coverage on business logic.

Tools

We use GitHub for all code hosting and pull requests. Linear is used for issue tracking. All CI/CD pipelines run on GitHub Actions.
excerpt: Learn how we conduct code reviews, what standards all engineers must follow, and how to give constructive feedback.
author: Engineering Team

title: Employee Benefits Guide 2024
content:
Overview

This guide covers all employee benefits available to full-time employees. Benefits become effective on your first day of employment unless otherwise noted.

Health Insurance

We offer three health plan tiers through our provider network. The company covers 80% of the premium for the employee and 50% for dependents. Enrollment must be completed within 30 days of your start date.

Dental and Vision

Dental and vision plans are available as add-ons. The company contributes $30/month toward dental and $15/month toward vision coverage.

Paid Time Off

Full-time employees receive 20 days of PTO per year, accrued monthly. PTO rolls over up to a maximum of 30 days. There is no payout of unused PTO upon separation.

Remote Work Policy

Employees may work remotely up to 3 days per week. Fully remote arrangements require manager approval and are reviewed annually. A $500 home office stipend is provided upon joining.

Learning and Development

Each employee has an annual $1,500 learning budget for courses, conferences, and books. Submit expenses through the Finance portal with a brief description of how it relates to your role.
excerpt: Complete guide to employee benefits including health insurance, PTO, remote work policy, and learning budgets.
author: People Operations

title: Incident Response Runbook
content:
Overview

This runbook defines the process for detecting, triaging, and resolving production incidents. All engineers on the on-call rotation must be familiar with this document.

Severity Levels

We classify incidents into four severity levels that determine response time and escalation paths.

SEV-1: Critical

Complete service outage or data loss. Response required within 15 minutes. Page the on-call engineer and notify the VP of Engineering immediately. All hands on deck.

SEV-2: High

Major feature unavailable or significant performance degradation affecting more than 20% of users. Response required within 30 minutes.

SEV-3: Medium

Non-critical feature degraded. Response required within 2 hours during business hours.

SEV-4: Low

Minor cosmetic issue or edge case. Can be addressed in normal sprint cycle.

Response Process

  • Acknowledge the alert in PagerDuty
  • Create a war room in Slack (#incident-response)
  • Post initial status update within 10 minutes
  • Identify the blast radius and stop the bleeding
  • Communicate with stakeholders every 30 minutes
  • Resolve and post a detailed post-mortem within 48 hours

Post-Mortem Requirements

Every SEV-1 and SEV-2 incident requires a blameless post-mortem document within 48 hours of resolution. The document must include a timeline, root cause analysis, impact assessment, and action items with owners and due dates.
excerpt: Step-by-step guide for detecting, triaging, and resolving production incidents including severity levels and post-mortem requirements.
author: Platform Team

title: Security Password and Access Policy
content:
Purpose

This policy defines the minimum security requirements for passwords, access management, and authentication across all company systems.

Password Requirements

All passwords must meet the following minimum standards to protect company systems and data.

  • Minimum 14 characters in length
  • Must include uppercase, lowercase, numbers, and symbols
  • Must not be reused within the last 12 passwords
  • Must be changed every 180 days for privileged accounts

Multi-Factor Authentication

MFA is mandatory for all company systems without exception. Hardware security keys (YubiKey) are preferred for privileged access. TOTP apps (Google Authenticator, Authy) are acceptable for standard access. SMS-based MFA is not permitted.

Access Management

Access follows the principle of least privilege. All access requests must be submitted through the IT Service Desk with manager approval. Access reviews are conducted quarterly for all systems. Access is revoked on the last day of employment.

Device Security

All company devices must have full-disk encryption enabled. Screen lock activates after 5 minutes of inactivity. Remote wipe capability must be enrolled via our MDM solution (Jamf for macOS, Intune for Windows) within 24 hours of device provisioning.
excerpt: Mandatory security policy covering password requirements, MFA enforcement, access management, and device security standards.
author: Security Team

title: Frontend Architecture Overview
content:
Overview

This document describes the frontend architecture used across all customer-facing products. Our frontend stack is built on Next.js with the App Router, TypeScript, and Tailwind CSS.

Technology Stack

  • Framework: Next.js 14 (App Router)
  • Language: TypeScript (strict mode)
  • Styling: Tailwind CSS
  • State Management: Zustand for global state, React Query for server state
  • Testing: Vitest + React Testing Library
  • CI/CD: GitHub Actions → Vercel

Project Structure

We follow a feature-based folder structure inside the app directory. Shared components live in /components, domain-specific components live alongside their routes, and all business logic is isolated in /lib directories.

Conventions

Component Guidelines

  • Use Server Components by default
  • Add 'use client' only when interactivity is required
  • Keep components under 200 lines — split when larger
  • Co-locate tests with components

Data Fetching

All data fetching happens in Server Components or Server Actions unless real-time updates are needed. We never fetch data in useEffect. React Query is used for client-side caching and mutations.

Performance Standards

All pages must score above 90 on Lighthouse for Performance and Accessibility. Core Web Vitals are monitored via Vercel Analytics. Bundle size is reviewed on every major release.
excerpt: Complete overview of our frontend architecture including the technology stack, project structure, conventions, and performance standards.
author: Frontend Platform
